title: Organic synthesis
summary: 9701 AS organic synthesis — multi-step routes, reagent choice, and functional group interconversions.
learningObjectives: Plan two- and three-step syntheses from given starting materials.
Use test-tube reactions to identify intermediates.
Apply knowledge of all AS functional group chemistry.

simpleExplanation.title: Designing a route
simpleExplanation.summary: Work backwards from the target molecule. Each step must change one functional group with known reagents.
steps: Alkene → alcohol: H₂O/H⁺ or cold dilute MnO₄⁻.
Carbonyl → acid: Fehling's/Tollens or further oxidation.

sections.content: **Organic synthesis** (21.1) is a capstone AS topic — examiners combine **mechanisms**, **tests**, and **reagent choice** in one flow chart question.
State **conditions** (heat, reflux, catalyst) alongside reagents — missing conditions often loses a mark per step.
